Okay, titles and promotions. So when you first start your business, it's pretty common that the roles are pretty jumbled and they don't really necessarily need to be well-defined, right? It's like, well everyone does everything, it works really well how we're doing it right now. I don't need titles because everyone already knows what they're responsible for, right? But as your business grows, this becomes the bottleneck to your communication if you do not change this, okay? And so why? Why do titles matter? Why would this become the bottleneck? Actually, you want to ask, right? Because most of the time business owners don't want to have to go do all that, okay? The fact of the matter is that one, employees want titles and two, people need to know who is who and how do you do that? You do that by having titles, right? And so one, people want titles. Why do they want titles? Because you as a business owner, you're going to be at your business until the end of time, right? But for the people that are working for you, this might not be where they want to end up and that being said, people want to take care of their future. How do they secure their future? They want a good resume. They want to show what they have accomplished, right? And so for them, they want the job to reflect how much hard work they're putting in. It's like if you bring someone in the beginning, you're calling them an admin, get they're managing all the rest of your team. They're doing the billing, they're doing the customer reachouts, they're in charge of payroll and then you look and you're like, oh, actually, they're an operations manager, right? It wouldn't be fitting to call them an admin because they're actually an operations manager. It's important that people have an appropriate title. What it is to them is it's really a representation of progress, work ethic and status for them. Okay. So that's why it's important. Number two is that people need to know who's who. So as you grow and as you most certainly do as you come through our program, you're going to have to add more staff members and as you add more staff numbers, everyone does not understand what everybody does. This is something that you have to accept as a business owner. You have a holistic view of the business. You see things, the full picture, right? You're like, oh, how do you not understand marketing and sales work together? Like, duh, how do you not understand how sales and the trainers work together? Like, how do you not see this? People don't because they're paying attention to their specific area. Okay. And more importantly, they don't know who to go to for certain things to get their job done, right? If sales doesn't understand that your operations manager is responsible for escalations, who are they to send the client to when they have an escalation that comes back to them because they're sales. They don't know. So then they just ignore it or send it to you, right? It's important that people have titles. And what you want to do is you want to think of a title as a shortcut for describing what each person does. Okay. So if you think of it like that, it makes it a lot easier. They're important to have and it will remove a lot of growing pains that you have as you grow. If you do have people with the appropriate titles as well as job descriptions.
